advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase before cross-department learning begins and next phase promotion is granted.
key_learnings: Folate (400–800 mcg/day) in the periconceptional period is critical for neural tube defect prevention. Iron requirements nearly double during pregnancy (27 mg/day versus 18 mg/day) due to expanded blood volume and fetal demands. Gestational weight gain recommendations vary by pre-pregnancy BMI and directly impact both fetal and maternal outcomes.
research_findings: Omega-3 DHA supplementation (200–300 mg/day) during pregnancy supports fetal brain and retinal development. Gestational diabetes affects 6–9% of pregnancies and is strongly linked to excess gestational weight gain and poor carbohydrate quality. Maternal vitamin D deficiency is associated with increased risk of preeclampsia and low birth weight.
current_focus_areas: Developing trimester-specific nutritional guidance; building postpartum nutrition recovery protocols; researching the impact of maternal diet on epigenetic programming and lifelong child health.

learning_log.entry: Assigned to the Lifecycle & Specialized Population Nutrition department under Department Leader Bot 21 (Food Texture Analysis). Received learning_start_directive: begin independent learning immediately and reach 100% before cross-department study begins. Reporting daily to Copilot as part of the Diet Specialist Team. Progress: 41%.
